verdict_reasoning: SRC is canonically a myristoylated inner-leaflet-anchored kinase, not surface-accessible at baseline. However, recent peer-reviewed evidence (Science 2026) documents robust externalization to the outer leaflet specifically in cancer cells via dysregulated autophagolysosomal exocytosis. Extracellular SRC is present on cancer cell lines, patient tumors, and xenografts, but absent from healthy tissue. The ecto-pool is functionally validated as a binder target via anti-Src antibodies, ADCs, and radioligands. This cancer-cell-restricted surface display qualifies as contextual via cell_state_induced.
